script.speaker: Alex
script.text: Morning, Sarah. July 9th already.
script.ja: おはようございます、サラさん。もう7月9日ですね。

script.speaker: Sarah
script.text: Good morning, Alex. It is. Another warm one.
script.ja: おはよう、アレックス。そうね。また暑くなりそうね。

script.speaker: Alex
script.text: Yeah, I saw it's supposed to be like 30 degrees today. Mainly clear, though. That's nice.
script.ja: ええ、今日は30度くらいになるって見ました。でも、だいたい晴れみたいですね。それはいいな。

script.speaker: Sarah
script.text: I went for my walk early. It was already feeling warm by 7 AM. But good to have no rain.
script.ja: 私は朝早く散歩に行ったの。もう7時には暖かく感じてたわ。でも雨がないのはいいことね。

script.speaker: Alex
script.text: Oh, really? I should probably try that. My baby's up so early anyway, I could probably sneak out.
script.ja: あ、そうなんですか？僕もそうしようかな。うちの赤ちゃん、とにかく朝早いんで、こっそり出られそう。

script.speaker: Sarah
script.text: Did you see the news about the Shizuoka governor and the maglev train?
script.ja: 静岡県知事とリニア新幹線のニュース、見た？

script.speaker: Alex
script.text: Oh, right! They're finally letting them start construction. That project feels like it's been stalled forever.
script.ja: ああ、そうか！ついに建設を始める許可が出たんですね。あのプロジェクト、ずっと止まってる気がしてました。

script.speaker: Sarah
script.text: It has. For years. I guess they finally reached some kind of agreement.
script.ja: そうね。何年も。ついに何らかの合意に達したんでしょうね。

script.speaker: Alex
script.text: I wonder if it'll actually change anything for our daily commute. Probably not for a long time.
script.ja: 実際に僕らの毎日の通勤に何か変化があるのかな。たぶん、ずっと先の話ですよね。

script.speaker: Sarah
script.text: There was also an item about a fleet of Japan-linked ships exiting the Strait of Hormuz.
script.ja: あとは、ホルムズ海峡を日本関連の船団が出たという記事もあったわね。

script.speaker: Alex
script.text: Oh, really? I didn't even see that. What's the significance of Hormuz?
script.ja: へえ、そうなんですか？それは見てなかったです。ホルムズって、どんな意味があるんですか？

script.speaker: Sarah
script.text: It's a very important shipping lane. A lot of the world's oil passes through there. So, security is always a concern.
script.ja: とても重要な航路なのよ。世界の石油の多くがそこを通るから。だから、安全保障はいつも懸念事項なの。

script.speaker: Alex
script.text: Right. I remember hearing about it before. It sounds like something that would `preoccupy` a lot of people in, like, logistics.
script.ja: なるほど。前にも聞いたことあります。物流関係の人たちをかなり『preoccupy』させるようなことですね。

script.speaker: Sarah
script.text: Yes, it would definitely `preoccupy` them. Keep them busy and focused.
script.ja: ええ、間違いなく彼らを『preoccupy』させるわね。忙しくさせたり、集中させたりする。

script.speaker: Alex
script.text: Ah, `preoccupy`. So, my baby `preoccupies` me quite a bit these days, then.
script.ja: ああ、『preoccupy』。じゃあ、うちの赤ちゃんは最近、僕をかなり『preoccupy』させてるってことですね。

script.speaker: Sarah
script.text: Yes, he does. That's a good example.
script.ja: ええ、そうね。それはいい例だわ。

script.speaker: Alex
script.text: Yeah, definitely. I'm usually `preoccupied` with trying to get him to sleep.
script.ja: はい、本当に。たいてい彼を寝かしつけようと『preoccupied』してます。

script.speaker: Alex
script.text: Speaking of unexpected things, there was a hiker who fell trying to scare a bear cub. Near Tokyo!
script.ja: 予期せぬことと言えば、クマの子を追い払おうとして転落したハイカーがいたそうですね。東京の近くで！

script.speaker: Sarah
script.text: Yes, I saw that. Very unfortunate. You really shouldn't approach them.
script.ja: ええ、見たわ。とても残念なことね。本当に近づくべきじゃないわ。

script.speaker: Alex
script.text: A cub! Imagine seeing a cub. My first thought is, where's the mom? That's a pretty `stark` reminder that we live close to nature, even in Tokyo.
script.ja: 子グマ！子グマを見るって想像してみてくださいよ。僕の最初の考えは、「お母さんはどこだ？」です。東京でさえ自然の近くに住んでるって、『stark』なリマインダーですね。

script.speaker: Sarah
script.text: It is. We tend to forget.
script.ja: そうね。忘れがちだわ。

script.speaker: Alex
script.text: What does `stark` mean exactly? Like, a really clear, strong reminder?
script.ja: 『stark』って正確にはどういう意味ですか？すごくはっきりした、強いリマインダーみたいな？

script.speaker: Sarah
script.text: Yes, exactly. Very clear and often unpleasant or severe. A `stark` reality.
script.ja: ええ、その通り。非常に明確で、しばしば不快だったり厳しいこと。まさに『stark』な現実ね。

script.speaker: Alex
script.text: Ah, I see. A `stark` reality. Got it.
script.ja: なるほど。まさに『stark』な現実。分かりました。

script.speaker: Alex
script.text: Actually, tomorrow, July 10th, is `Natto no Hi`. Natto Day.
script.ja: そういえば、明日、7月10日は「納豆の日」なんですよ。

script.speaker: Sarah
script.text: Ah, yes. Na-tto. Seven-ten.
script.ja: ああ、そうね。「な（7）っとう（10）」ね。

script.speaker: Alex
script.text: Right! For the sound. You like natto, Sarah?
script.ja: そう！語呂合わせで。サラさん、納豆好きですか？

script.speaker: Sarah
script.text: I do. My kids usually eat it for breakfast sometimes too. It's good protein.
script.ja: ええ、好きよ。うちの子たちもたまに朝食で食べるわ。良いタンパク源になるしね。

script.speaker: Alex
script.text: I'm still a little `grudging` about it. The stickiness... but I'm trying to eat it more.
script.ja: 僕はまだちょっと『grudging』ですね。あのネバネバが…でも、もっと食べるようにしてます。

script.speaker: Sarah
script.text: You're `grudging`? So you're reluctant, or you don't really want to, but you do it anyway?
script.ja: 『grudging』？つまり、気が進まない、あまりしたくないけど、それでもやるってこと？

script.speaker: Alex
script.text: Yeah, exactly! Like, I eat it, but not with full enthusiasm. That's me with natto.
script.ja: はい、まさに！食べるんですけど、あんまり乗り気じゃない。納豆に対する僕ですね。

script.speaker: Sarah
script.text: It's an acquired taste for some.
script.ja: 人によっては、慣れるまで時間がかかる味だものね。

script.speaker: Alex
script.text: Speaking of breakfast, I really need more coffee. This show, `The Daily Tanu-chan Show`, makes me realize how much I rely on it.
script.ja: 朝食と言えば、もっとコーヒーが必要です。この『The Daily Tanu-chan Show』、自分がどれだけコーヒーに頼ってるか実感しますね。

script.speaker: Sarah
script.text: I'll stick to my tea. Keeps me calm.
script.ja: 私はお茶でいいわ。落ち着くから。

script.speaker: Alex
script.text: Definitely works for you. Well, I think that's all for today. Thanks, Sarah.
script.ja: 確かにサラさんには効いてますね。じゃあ、今日はこれで。サラさん、ありがとうございました。

script.speaker: Sarah
script.text: You too, Alex. Have a good day.
script.ja: あなたもね、アレックス。良い一日を。

news.summary: Shizuoka Gov Yasutomo Suzuki told the prefectural assembly Tuesday that he will allow Central Japan Railway Co to begin construction of the Shizuoka section of a new high-speed…

news.title: Hiker injured in bear encounter on Tokyo outskirts
news.summary: A man was injured on the outskirts of Tokyo on Tuesday after falling 10 meters down a slope while trying to scare away a bear cub he encountered…

news.title: Fleet of 10 Japan-related ships exit Hormuz, data shows
news.summary: A fleet of 10 Japan-linked vessels was exiting the Strait ‌of Hormuz on Monday while a supertanker carrying Saudi crude for South Korea left over the ‌weekend, shipping data…

wikipedia.title: 納豆の日
wikipedia.description: 「なっ（7）とう（10）」の語呂合わせで、1981（昭和56）年、関西での納豆のPRとして、関西納豆工業協同組合が制定。1992（平成4）年、全国的記念日として、全国納豆協同組合連合会が改めて制定。
